Require Import Coq.ZArith.ZArith.
Require Import Coq.Bool.Bool.
Require Import Coq.Strings.String.
Require Import Coq.Strings.Ascii.
Require Import Coq.Lists.List.
Require Import Coq.Classes.RelationClasses.
Require Import Coq.Classes.Morphisms.
Require Import Coq.micromega.Psatz.
Require Import Coq.Sorting.Permutation.
From AUXLib Require Import int_auto Axioms Feq Idents ListLib VMap.
Require Import SetsClass.SetsClass. Import SetsNotation.
From SimpleC.SL Require Import Mem SeparationLogic.
From SimpleC.EE.CAV.verify_20260607_140907_p122_add_elements Require Import p122_add_elements_goal.
Require Import Logic.LogicGenerator.demo932.Interface.
Local Open Scope Z_scope.
Local Open Scope sets.
Local Open Scope string_scope.
Local Open Scope list.
Import naive_C_Rules.
Require Import p122_add_elements.

(* Helper lemmas -- placed before Local Open Scope sac to avoid sac-scope notation conflicts *)

Lemma two_digit_bool_equiv : forall x : Z,
  Z.leb (-99) x && Z.leb x 99 = is_at_most_two_digits x.
Proof.
  intro x. unfold is_at_most_two_digits.
  case_eq (Z.leb (-99) x); case_eq (Z.leb x 99);
  case_eq (Z.ltb (-100) x); case_eq (Z.ltb x 100);
  intros H1 H2 H3 H4; try reflexivity;
  rewrite ?Z.leb_le, ?Z.leb_nle, ?Z.ltb_lt, ?Z.ltb_ge in *; lia.
Qed.

Lemma sum_two_digit_upto_step : forall (i : Z) (l : list Z),
  0 <= i ->
  sum_two_digit_upto (i + 1) l =
  if Z.leb (-99) (Znth i l 0) && Z.leb (Znth i l 0) 99
  then sum_two_digit_upto i l + Znth i l 0
  else sum_two_digit_upto i l.
Proof.
  intros i l Hi.
  unfold sum_two_digit_upto.
  replace (Z.to_nat (i + 1)) with (S (Z.to_nat i)).
  2: { replace (i + 1) with (Z.succ i) by lia.
       rewrite Z2Nat.inj_succ by lia. reflexivity. }
  simpl (sum_two_digit_upto_nat (S (Z.to_nat i)) l).
  rewrite Z2Nat.id by lia.
  reflexivity.
Qed.

(* Prove with Z.leb form directly to avoid rewriting under filter binders *)
Lemma sum_two_digit_upto_nat_eq_fold_leb : forall (n : nat) (l : list Z),
  sum_two_digit_upto_nat n l =
  fold_left Z.add (filter (fun x => Z.leb (-99) x && Z.leb x 99) (firstn n l)) 0.
Proof.
  induction n; intros l.
  - simpl. reflexivity.
  - simpl (sum_two_digit_upto_nat (S n) l).
    rewrite IHn.
    destruct (le_lt_dec (length l) n) as [Hge | Hlt].
    + assert (Hf1: firstn n l = l) by (apply firstn_all2; lia).
      assert (Hf2: firstn (S n) l = l) by (apply firstn_all2; lia).
      assert (Hz : Znth (Z.of_nat n) l 0 = 0).
      { unfold Znth.
        destruct (Z_lt_dec (Z.of_nat n) 0) as [Hn | Hn]. { lia. }
        rewrite Nat2Z.id.
        apply nth_overflow. lia. }
      rewrite Hf1, Hf2, Hz.
      destruct (Z.leb (-99) 0 && Z.leb 0 99); ring.
    + assert (Hznth : Znth (Z.of_nat n) l 0 = nth n l 0).
      { unfold Znth.
        destruct (Z_lt_dec (Z.of_nat n) 0) as [Hn | Hn]. { lia. }
        rewrite Nat2Z.id. reflexivity. }
      rewrite Hznth.
      assert (Hfirstn : firstn (S n) l = firstn n l ++ (nth n l 0 :: nil)).
      { clear - Hlt.
        revert n Hlt.
        induction l as [|a t IH]; intros n Hlt.
        - simpl in Hlt. lia.
        - destruct n as [|n'].
          + simpl. reflexivity.
          + simpl. f_equal. apply IH. simpl in Hlt. lia. }
      rewrite Hfirstn.
      rewrite filter_app.
      rewrite fold_left_app.
      simpl (filter (fun x => Z.leb (-99) x && Z.leb x 99) (nth n l 0 :: nil)).
      destruct (Z.leb (-99) (nth n l 0) && Z.leb (nth n l 0) 99) eqn:Hc.
      * simpl. ring.
      * simpl. reflexivity.
Qed.

Lemma sum_two_digit_upto_nat_eq_fold : forall (n : nat) (l : list Z),
  sum_two_digit_upto_nat n l =
  fold_left Z.add (filter is_at_most_two_digits (firstn n l)) 0.
Proof.
  intros n l.
  rewrite sum_two_digit_upto_nat_eq_fold_leb.
  f_equal.
  apply filter_ext.
  intro x. apply two_digit_bool_equiv.
Qed.

Lemma sum_two_digit_upto_eq_fold : forall (k : Z) (l : list Z),
  0 <= k ->
  sum_two_digit_upto k l =
  fold_left Z.add (filter is_at_most_two_digits (sublist 0 k l)) 0.
Proof.
  intros k l Hk.
  unfold sum_two_digit_upto, sublist.
  simpl (Z.to_nat 0).
  simpl (skipn 0 (firstn (Z.to_nat k) l)).
  apply sum_two_digit_upto_nat_eq_fold.
Qed.

Local Open Scope sac.

Lemma proof_of_p122_add_elements_safety_wit_6 : p122_add_elements_safety_wit_6.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pures; dump_pre_spatial;
  match goal with
  | Hrange : sum_two_digit_int_range ?kp ?lv2,
    Hi : 0 <= ?ii, Hlt : ?ii < ?kp,
    Hs : ?ss = sum_two_digit_upto ?ii ?lv2 |- _ =>
    pose proof (Hrange ii Hi Hlt) as [[? ?] [Hmin Hmax]]; rewrite Hs; lia
  end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p122_add_elements_entail_wit_1 : p122_add_elements_entail_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p122_add_elements_entail_wit_2_1 : p122_add_elements_entail_wit_2_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* remaining: s + Znth i lv 0 = sum_two_digit_upto (i+1) lv *)
  rewrite sum_two_digit_upto_step by lia.
  (* assert each leb separately to avoid sac-scope && conflict *)
  assert (Hle1: Z.leb (-99) (Znth i lv 0) = true) by (apply Z.leb_le; lia).
  assert (Hle2: Z.leb (Znth i lv 0) 99 = true) by (apply Z.leb_le; lia).
  rewrite Hle1, Hle2. simpl. lia.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p122_add_elements_entail_wit_2_2 : p122_add_elements_entail_wit_2_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* remaining: s = sum_two_digit_upto (i+1) lv *)
  rewrite sum_two_digit_upto_step by lia.
  assert (Hle: Z.leb (-99) (Znth i lv 0) = false)
    by (rewrite <- Bool.not_true_iff_false; rewrite Z.leb_le; lia).
  rewrite Hle. simpl. lia.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p122_add_elements_entail_wit_2_3 : p122_add_elements_entail_wit_2_3.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* remaining: s = sum_two_digit_upto (i+1) lv *)
  rewrite sum_two_digit_upto_step by lia.
  assert (Hle: Z.leb (Znth i lv 0) 99 = false)
    by (rewrite <- Bool.not_true_iff_false; rewrite Z.leb_le; lia).
  rewrite Hle, Bool.andb_false_r. simpl. lia.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p122_add_elements_return_wit_1 : p122_add_elements_return_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  assert (Heq : i = k_pre) by lia.
  subst i.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* remaining: problem_122_spec lv k_pre s *)
  unfold problem_122_spec.
  rewrite <- sum_two_digit_upto_eq_fold by lia.
  assumption.
Qed.

content.thinking:
I now have a complete understanding of the proof style. Let me now write the proof for `proof_manual.v` for p072.

Key things I notice from the p122 example:
1. Helper lemmas are placed before `Local Open Scope sac`
2. Main lemmas use `pre_process` first, then `entailer!` or `split_pure_spatial`
3. `entailer!` handles many goals automatically
4. `dump_pre_spatial` removes spatial assumptions from the context (they get discharged separately)
Now I'm identifying the key helper lemmas I'll need: one that relates partial sums to list slicing, another connecting fold operations to summation, and a third handling list reversal properties.

For the reversal lemma, I need to establish that if elements at symmetric positions are equal, the list equals its own reverse, which relies on proving that the nth element of a reversed list corresponds to the symmetric position in the original. I should verify what's available in the standard library for length preservation under reversal and how Zlength relates to these operations. Now I'm working through the relationship between `fold_left` and `fold_right` for summing a list, establishing that they're equivalent through induction on the list structure. Then I'm starting to examine the `will_it_fly_int_range` predicate definition.

Continuing with the proof for `safety_wit_7`, I need to show that the sum of a sublist plus the next element stays within integer bounds. The key is applying `will_it_fly_int_range` at position `i+1` by verifying the bounds hold, then using a lemma about how sublists compose when stepping through the list.

For `entail_wit_1`, the base case is straightforward: a sublist from 0 to 0 is empty, so its sum is 0, and the universal quantifier over an empty range is vacuously true.

For `entail_wit_2`, I need to establish that at step i+1, the bounds hold (0 ≤ i+1 ≤ q_size_pre), the symmetry property extends to include the new element (using the inductive hypothesis for earlier indices and the given hypothesis for k=i), and the sum relationship follows from the sublist stepping lemma combined with the inductive assumption about s.

For `return_wit_1`, I'm evaluating the problem specification at the final state where i equals q_size_pre, which simplifies the condition to checking that the list is a palindrome and its sum doesn't exceed the weight limit.

For `return_wit_2`, I need to show the negation of the palindrome and sum condition holds at the initial state, which requires demonstrating that either the list isn't a palindrome or the sum exceeds the bound.

For `return_wit_3`, I'm working through the case where specific elements at symmetric positions differ, which directly establishes that the list cannot be a palindrome.

Writing Coq proof... Proving that accessing the k-th element of a reversed list equals accessing the (length - 1 - k)-th element of the original list, using properties of Znth and list reversal. Finishing the proof for the mirror property... Proving the length equality and then using the reverse lemma to show that each element at index i in the reversed list matches the corresponding element in the original list through the mirror property. Writing the Coq proof... Writing the lemma proof... Working through the arithmetic constraints for the `Z.to_nat` conversion—I need to verify that `Z.of_nat (length l) - 1 - k` stays non-negative given that `k < length l`, then simplify the nested subtraction and conversion operations.

Let me try a cleaner approach using `omega` or `lia` to handle the arithmetic automatically, or work directly with the natural number version of the lemma to avoid these integer conversion headaches. Finishing the proof with the correct lemmas... Trying a cleaner approach with `Z2Nat.inj_sub` to handle the nested subtraction, though I need to verify the exact form of that lemma and whether it applies directly to this nested case. Applying the `Z2Nat.inj_sub` lemma to simplify the subtraction of natural numbers converted to integers, then using `Nat2Z.id` to convert back and simplify the constants. Applying the subtraction lemmas to break down the right side into separate terms, then simplifying the integer-to-natural conversions to match the left side `length l - 1 - Z.to_nat k`, which should let `lia` finish the proof once I verify the intermediate forms align correctly. Finishing the proof with case analysis...

I'm handling the case where `k >= 0` by destructing on whether `Zlength l - 1 - k` is negative, then applying the `rev_nth` lemma to relate the reversed list access to the original list. The proof uses length arithmetic to show the indices match up correctly, converting between `Z` and `nat` representations and simplifying the resulting expressions. I realize `f_equal` won't work cleanly here since both sides are natural number expressions, so I need to be more careful with the equality reasoning. Applying the `rev_nth` lemma, I get `nth (length l - S (Z.to_nat k)) l 0` on the left side, and I need to show this equals `nth (Z.to_nat (Zlength l - 1 - k)) l 0` on the right, so the key is proving `length l - S (Z.to_nat k) = Z.to_nat (Zlength l - 1 - k)`.

file.content:
Lemma sublist_self:
  forall (l1: list A) x,
    x = Zlength l1 ->
    sublist 0 x l1 = l1.
Proof.
  intros. unfold sublist; subst.
  rewrite skipn_O. rewrite Zlength_correct.
  replace (Z.to_nat (Z.of_nat (length l1))) with (length l1) by lia.
  apply firstn_all.
Qed.

Lemma Zlength_sublist':
  forall (l: list A) i j,
    Zlength (sublist i j l) = 
    Z.of_nat (min (Z.to_nat j) (length l) - Z.to_nat i).
Proof.
  intros.
  rewrite Zlength_correct.
  unfold sublist.
  rewrite length_skipn.
  rewrite length_firstn.
  reflexivity.
Qed.

Lemma sublist_split_app_r:
  forall lo hi len (l1 l2: list A),
    Zlength l1 = len ->
    len <= lo <= hi ->
    sublist lo hi (l1 ++ l2) = sublist (lo - len) (hi - len) l2.
Proof.

  match l with 
  | nil => Tac 
  | ?x :: ?l' => 
    let a := fresh "v" in
    match x with 
      | norm_asrt (“ ?B ”) => pose (a := B) ; change B with a

  end. 

Ltac Rename Tac:=

    let L2 := All_listasrts Q in
    let l := eval cbn [List.app] in (List.app L1 L2) in
      Rename_rec l Tac

Ltac dump_pre_spatial :=
  apply dump_spatial_left.

Lemma split_pure_and_spatial_goals : forall P pure spatial,
  P |-- spatial -> P |-- pure -> P |-- pure && spatial.
Proof.
  intros. unfold andp, derivable1 in *. intros. split; auto.
Qed.

Ltac _assert_pure t :=
  lazymatch t with
  | “ _ ” => idtac
  | ?A && ?B => _assert_pure A; _assert_pure B
  | _ => fail "not pure: RHS contains non-“ _ ” parts"
  end.

Ltac _assert_no_pure t :=
  lazymatch t with
  | “ _ ” => fail "spatial part contains a pure “ _ ”"
  | ?A ** ?B => _assert_no_pure A; _assert_no_pure B
  | ?A && ?B => _assert_no_pure A; _assert_no_pure B
  | _ => idtac
  end.

Ltac split_pure_spatial :=
  normalize;
  asrt_simpl_pure;
  try repeat rewrite <- logic_equiv_andp_assoc;
  match goal with
  | |- _ |-- (?A && ?B) =>

Require Import Coq.ZArith.ZArith.
Require Import Coq.Bool.Bool.
Require Import Coq.Strings.String.
Require Import Coq.Strings.Ascii.
Require Import Coq.Lists.List.
Require Import Coq.Classes.RelationClasses.
Require Import Coq.Classes.Morphisms.
Require Import Coq.micromega.Psatz.
Require Import Coq.Sorting.Permutation.
From AUXLib Require Import int_auto Axioms Feq Idents ListLib VMap.
Require Import SetsClass.SetsClass. Import SetsNotation.
From SimpleC.SL Require Import Mem SeparationLogic.
From SimpleC.EE.CAV.verify_20260607_152224_p072_will_it_fly Require Import p072_will_it_fly_goal.
Require Import Logic.LogicGenerator.demo932.Interface.
Local Open Scope Z_scope.
Local Open Scope sets.
Local Open Scope string_scope.
Local Open Scope list.
Import naive_C_Rules.
Require Import p072_will_it_fly.

(* Helper lemmas placed before Local Open Scope sac to avoid notation conflicts *)

(* fold_left with addition equals sum (fold_right) with accumulator *)
Lemma fold_left_Z_add_acc : forall (l : list Z) (acc : Z),
  fold_left (fun a x => a + x) l acc = acc + sum l.
Proof.
  induction l as [|a t IH]; intros acc.
  - simpl. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
  - simpl. rewrite IH. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* sum of sublist 0 (i+1) = sum of sublist 0 i + Znth i *)
Lemma sum_sublist_step : forall (lv : list Z) (i : Z),
  0 <= i < Zlength lv ->
  sum (sublist 0 (i + 1) lv) = sum (sublist 0 i lv) + Znth i lv 0.
Proof.
  intros lv i Hi.
  rewrite (sublist_split 0 (i + 1) i lv) by lia.
  rewrite (sublist_single 0 i lv) by lia.
  rewrite sum_app.
  unfold sum at 2. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* Znth of rev list equals Znth from the other end *)
Lemma Znth_rev_lem : forall (l : list Z) (k : Z),
  0 <= k < Zlength l ->
  Znth k (rev l) 0 = Znth (Zlength l - 1 - k) l 0.
Proof.
  intros l k Hk.
  unfold Znth.
  destruct (Z_lt_dec k 0). { lia. }
  destruct (Z_lt_dec (Zlength l - 1 - k) 0).
  { rewrite Zlength_correct in *. lia. }
  rewrite rev_nth by (rewrite Zlength_correct in Hk; lia).
  f_equal.
  rewrite Zlength_correct.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Nat2Z.id.
  simpl (Z.to_nat 1).
  lia.
Qed.

(* mirror predicate (pointwise palindrome) implies list = rev list *)
Lemma mirror_to_rev : forall (lv : list Z),
  (forall k, 0 <= k < Zlength lv -> Znth k lv 0 = Znth (Zlength lv - 1 - k) lv 0) ->
  lv = rev lv.
Proof.
  intros lv Hmirror.
  rewrite (list_eq_ext lv (rev lv) 0).
  split.
  - rewrite !Zlength_correct. rewrite rev_length. reflexivity.
  - intros i Hi.
    rewrite Znth_rev_lem by exact Hi.
    apply Hmirror. exact Hi.
Qed.

Local Open Scope sac.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_safety_wit_7 : p072_will_it_fly_safety_wit_7.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pures; dump_pre_spatial;
  match goal with
  | Hwifr : will_it_fly_int_range lv,
    Hilen : i < q_size_pre,
    Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
    Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv) |- _ =>
    pose proof (sum_sublist_step lv i ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as Hstep;
    pose proof (Hwifr (i + 1) ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as [Hlo Hhi];
    linarith
  end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_1 : p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures.
    all: dump_pre_spatial.
    all: try lia.
    all: try assumption.
    all: try (intros k [? ?]; lia).
    all: try (change (sublist 0 0 lv) with (@nil Z); unfold sum; simpl; lia).
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2 : p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures.
    all: dump_pre_spatial.
    all: try lia.
    all: try assumption.
    (* extended palindrome invariant *)
    + intros k [Hk1 Hk2].
      destruct (Z.eq_dec k i) as [Heq | Hne].
      * subst k. assumption.
      * match goal with
        | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < i) ->
              Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0 |- _ =>
          apply Hmirror; split; lia
        end.
    (* sum step *)
    + match goal with
      | Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv),
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
        Hilt : i < q_size_pre |- _ =>
        rewrite sum_sublist_step by (rewrite <- Hqlen; lia);
        linarith
      end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_1 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.

    + intro _.

        intros k Hk.

        | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < q_size_pre) ->
              Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0,
          Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv |- _ =>

      * rewrite fold_left_Z_add_acc.
        match goal with

          Hsle : s <= w_pre |- _ =>
          rewrite Hs, (sublist_self lv q_size_pre Hqlen); lia
        end.
    + intros _. reflexivity.
Qed.

  - cancel.
  - split_pures; dump_pre_spatial.

(* Helper lemmas placed before Local Open Scope sac to avoid notation conflicts *)

(* fold_left with addition equals sum (fold_right) with accumulator *)
Lemma fold_left_Z_add_acc : forall (l : list Z) (acc : Z),
  fold_left (fun a x => a + x) l acc = acc + sum l.
Proof.
  induction l as [|a t IH]; intros acc.
  - simpl. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
  - simpl. rewrite IH. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* sum of sublist 0 (i+1) = sum of sublist 0 i + Znth i *)
Lemma sum_sublist_step : forall (lv : list Z) (i : Z),
  0 <= i < Zlength lv ->
  sum (sublist 0 (i + 1) lv) = sum (sublist 0 i lv) + Znth i lv 0.
Proof.
  intros lv i Hi.
  rewrite (sublist_split 0 (i + 1) i lv) by lia.
  rewrite (sublist_single 0 i lv) by lia.
  rewrite sum_app.
  unfold sum at 2. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* Znth of rev list equals Znth from the other end *)
Lemma Znth_rev_lem : forall (l : list Z) (k : Z),
  0 <= k < Zlength l ->
  Znth k (rev l) 0 = Znth (Zlength l - 1 - k) l 0.
Proof.
  intros l k Hk.
  unfold Znth.
  destruct (Z_lt_dec k 0). { lia. }
  destruct (Z_lt_dec (Zlength l - 1 - k) 0).
  { rewrite Zlength_correct in *. lia. }
  rewrite rev_nth by (rewrite Zlength_correct in Hk; lia).
  f_equal.
  rewrite Zlength_correct.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Nat2Z.id.
  simpl (Z.to_nat 1).
  lia.
Qed.

(* mirror predicate (pointwise palindrome) implies list = rev list *)
Lemma mirror_to_rev : forall (lv : list Z),
  (forall k, 0 <= k < Zlength lv -> Znth k lv 0 = Znth (Zlength lv - 1 - k) lv 0) ->
  lv = rev lv.
Proof.
  intros lv Hmirror.
  rewrite (list_eq_ext lv (rev lv) 0).
  split.
  - rewrite !Zlength_correct. rewrite rev_length. reflexivity.
  - intros i Hi.
    rewrite Znth_rev_lem by exact Hi.
    apply Hmirror. exact Hi.
Qed.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Deterministic QCP ``coqc`` invocation.

Compiles a single ``.v`` under the QualifiedCProgramming load-path so callers can
run Coq's own reduction machinery on closed terms. coqc runs with
``QualifiedCProgramming/SeparationLogic`` as its working directory and the base
``-R`` set below resolves every QCP library a generated ``.v`` may ``Require``.

Primary use: ``run_eval`` discharges *computable* ``needs_judge`` clauses by
generating a tiny ``.v`` with ``Eval vm_compute in <closed_term>.`` and reading
the reduced normal form off stdout — Coq reduction of a closed term, not C
execution and not a proof. The same helper can later back audit's compile
replay cross-check.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess


# This module lives at <repo>/scripts/, so the repo root is one level up.
REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
QCP_SL_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "QualifiedCProgramming" / "SeparationLogic"

# Base load-path, relative to QCP_SL_DIR.
BASE_LOAD_PATH: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
    ("SeparationLogic", "SimpleC.SL"),
    ("unifysl", "Logic"),
    ("sets", "SetsClass"),
    ("compcert_lib", "compcert.lib"),
    ("auxlibs", "AUXLib"),
    ("examples", "SimpleC.EE"),
    ("StrategyLib", "SimpleC.StrategyLib"),
    ("Common", "SimpleC.Common"),
    ("fixedpoints", "FP"),
    ("MonadLib", "MonadLib"),
    ("listlib", "ListLib"),
]


# Coq compile intermediates. `.aux` covers both `name.aux` and the hidden
# `.name.aux` Coq emits (its suffix is still `.aux`).
COMPILE_ARTIFACT_SUFFIXES: tuple[str, ...] = (".vo", ".vok", ".vos", ".glob", ".aux")


def coqc_bin() -> str:
    """coqc binary; override with ``$COQC`` (e.g. an absolute opam path)."""
    return os.environ.get("COQC", "coqc")


def clean_compile_artifacts(directory: Path, *, recursive: bool = True) -> list[str]:
    """Delete Coq compile intermediates under ``directory``; keep ``.v``/``.c``.

    Use only on workspace dirs (``coq/generated``, ``evaluation/compute``, an
    audit ``verified/`` copy) — never on the shared QCP tree, whose prebuilt
    ``examples/*.vo`` must survive. Returns the paths removed.
    """
    d = Path(directory)
    if not d.exists():
        return []
    removed: list[str] = []
    for p in (d.rglob("*") if recursive else d.iterdir()):
        if p.is_file() and p.suffix in COMPILE_ARTIFACT_SUFFIXES:
            try:
                p.unlink()
                removed.append(str(p))
            except OSError:
                pass
    return removed


def _base_args() -> list[str]:
    args: list[str] = []
    for path, prefix in BASE_LOAD_PATH:
        args += ["-R", path, prefix]
    return args


def build_command(
    v_file: Path,
    *,
    extra_r: list[tuple[str, str]] | None = None,
    extra_q: list[tuple[str, str]] | None = None,
    coqc: str | None = None,
) -> list[str]:
    """Assemble the coqc argv for ``v_file`` (resolved absolute).

    ``extra_q`` are added as ``-Q`` (e.g. a problem's ``original/`` dir with the
    empty prefix); ``extra_r`` as ``-R`` (e.g. a workspace ``generated/`` dir

content.content:
90	    empty prefix); ``extra_r`` as ``-R`` (e.g. a workspace ``generated/`` dir
91	    with its ``SimpleC.EE.CAV.*`` prefix). Both come before the file, after BASE.
92	    """
93	    cmd = [coqc or coqc_bin(), *_base_args()]
94	    for path, prefix in (extra_q or []):
95	        cmd += ["-Q", str(Path(path).resolve()), prefix]
96	    for path, prefix in (extra_r or []):
97	        cmd += ["-R", str(Path(path).resolve()), prefix]
98	    cmd.append(str(Path(v_file).resolve()))
99	    return cmd
100	
101	
102	def run_coqc(

empty prefix); ``extra_r`` as ``-R`` (e.g. a workspace ``generated/`` dir
    with its ``SimpleC.EE.CAV.*`` prefix). Both come before the file, after BASE.
    """
    cmd = [coqc or coqc_bin(), *_base_args()]
    for path, prefix in (extra_q or []):
        cmd += ["-Q", str(Path(path).resolve()), prefix]
    for path, prefix in (extra_r or []):
        cmd += ["-R", str(Path(path).resolve()), prefix]
    cmd.append(str(Path(v_file).resolve()))
    return cmd


def run_coqc(
    v_file: Path,
    *,
    extra_r: list[tuple[str, str]] | None = None,
    extra_q: list[tuple[str, str]] | None = None,
    coqc: str | None = None,
    env: dict[str, str] | None = None,
    timeout_seconds: int = 300,
) -> tuple[int, str, str]:
    """Compile ``v_file`` under the QCP load-path. Return ``(rc, stdout, stderr)``.

    cwd is ``QualifiedCProgramming/SeparationLogic`` so the relative BASE ``-R``
    paths resolve. On timeout returns ``(124, "", <message>)``.
    """
    cmd = build_command(v_file, extra_r=extra_r, extra_q=extra_q, coqc=coqc)
    try:
        proc = subprocess.run(
            cmd,
            cwd=QCP_SL_DIR,
            env=env or os.environ.copy(),
            text=True,
            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
            stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
            timeout=timeout_seconds,
        )
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        return 124, "", f"coqc timed out after {timeout_seconds}s"
    except OSError as exc:
        return 127, "", f"coqc not runnable: {exc}"
    return proc.returncode, proc.stdout, proc.stderr


def parse_eval_normal_form(stdout: str) -> str | None:
    """Extract the reduced term printed by ``Eval vm_compute in ...``.

    Coq prints ``     = <value>`` then ``     : <type>``. Returns ``<value>``
    (possibly multi-line, joined) for the last such block, or None.
    """
    lines = stdout.splitlines()
    value_lines: list[str] | None = None
    collecting: list[str] | None = None
    for raw in lines:
        stripped = raw.strip()
        if stripped.startswith("="):
            collecting = [stripped[1:].strip()]
        elif collecting is not None and stripped.startswith(":"):
            value_lines = collecting
            collecting = None

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Re-run symexec WITHOUT throwing away already-proven witnesses.

Plain `symexec` deletes and regenerates proof_manual.v with every witness back
to `Admitted` — so a retry that re-runs symexec wipes all the work already done.
This wrapper preserves it:

  1. snapshot the current proof_manual.v: keep each proven (`Qed.`/`Defined.`)
     `proof_of_<func>_<wit>` block, plus any agent-added helper lemmas; record
     each witness's VC body from goal.v.
  2. run the real symexec (canonical symexec command, same as run_verify.run_symexec).
  3. for each freshly-generated witness whose VC body in the NEW goal.v is
     byte-identical to the OLD one, splice the old proof back in (PROOF §24:
     only reuse when the VC body is unchanged); re-insert helper lemmas.
  4. witnesses whose VC changed, or that were never proven, stay `Admitted`.

On symexec failure the original generated files are restored, so the workspace
is never left worse than before.

Usage (drop-in for the canonical symexec command):
  python3 scripts/symexec_keep_proofs.py --name <NAME> --stamp <STAMP>
"""
import argparse, re, shutil, subprocess, sys, tempfile, time
from pathlib import Path

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
SYMEXEC_BIN = REPO_ROOT / "QualifiedCProgramming" / "linux-binary" / "symexec"
GEN_SUFFIXES = ("goal", "proof_auto", "proof_manual", "goal_check", "proof_check")


def log_phase(workspace: Path, event: str, detail: str = "") -> None:
    """Append a timestamped phase event to <ws>/logs/phase_timeline.tsv.

    Each symexec rerun bounds one annotation↔proof cycle; run_verify aggregates
    these into per-cycle durations for metrics. Best-effort: never raises.
    """
    try:
        line = f"{time.time():.3f}\t{time.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}\t{event}\t{detail}\n"
        p = workspace / "logs" / "phase_timeline.tsv"
        p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        with open(p, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            f.write(line)
    except Exception:
        pass

ITEM_BOUNDARY = re.compile(r"(?m)^(?=(?:Lemma|Theorem|Corollary|Definition|Fixpoint)\s)")
NAME_RE = re.compile(r"^(?:Lemma|Theorem|Corollary|Definition|Fixpoint)\s+([A-Za-z_][\w']*)")
DEF_BOUNDARY = re.compile(r"(?m)^(?=Definition\s)")


def _norm(s: str) -> str:
    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()


def split_items(text: str):
    """Return (header, [item_text, ...]) split on top-level decl boundaries."""
    parts = ITEM_BOUNDARY.split(text)
    return parts[0], parts[1:]

def compile_generated(workspace_path: Path, function_name: str, input_v_path: Path | None, timeout_seconds: int = 120) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
    generated_dir = workspace_path / "coq" / "generated"
    original_dir = workspace_path / "original"
    logic_path = f"SimpleC.EE.CAV.{workspace_path.name}"
    removed_generated_junk = clean_generated_non_v_entries(generated_dir)
    deps_dir, dep_logs = prepare_strategy_deps(generated_dir)
    extra_q = []
    if deps_dir is not None:
        extra_q.append((str(deps_dir), ""))
    extra_q.append((str(original_dir), ""))
    extra_r = [(str(generated_dir), logic_path)]
    logs: list[str] = []
    logs.extend(f"removed generated non-v entry: {entry}" for entry in removed_generated_junk)
    logs.extend(dep_logs)

    try:
        if deps_dir is not None:
            ok, dep_compile_logs = compile_strategy_deps(deps_dir, timeout_seconds)
            logs.extend(dep_compile_logs)
            if not ok:
                return False, logs

        for original_v in sorted(original_dir.glob("*.v")):
            rc, out, err = coq_runner.run_coqc(original_v, extra_q=extra_q, timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds)
            logs.append(f"$ coqc {original_v}\nrc={rc}\n{out}{err}")
            if rc != 0:
                return False, logs

        for suffix in ("goal", "proof_auto", "proof_manual", "goal_check"):
            path = generated_dir / f"{function_name}_{suffix}.v"
            rc, out, err = coq_runner.run_coqc(path, extra_q=extra_q, extra_r=extra_r, timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds)
            logs.append(f"$ coqc {path}\nrc={rc}\n{out}{err}")
            if rc != 0:
                return False, logs
        return True, logs
    finally:
        coq_runner.clean_compile_artifacts(original_dir, recursive=False)
        coq_runner.clean_compile_artifacts(generated_dir, recursive=False)

But `lia` can handle non-trivial linear arithmetic goals... but can it handle goals involving `sum (sublist 0 (i+1) lv)` as an atom? Actually, lia works purely on linear arithmetic with Z variables. If `sum (sublist 0 (i+1) lv)` appears as an uninterpreted atom, lia treats it as a Z variable. 

Now I have the key hypotheses: the step equation relating the sum at position i+1 to the sum at position i plus the element at i, the substitution for s, and the bounds on the sum at i+1. From these I need to derive the goal about s plus the element staying within INT_MAX and INT_MIN.

The algebraic chain is straightforward: substituting s with its definition gives me `sum (sublist 0 i lv) + Znth i lv 0`, which by the step equation equals `sum (sublist 0 (i+1) lv)`, and I already have that this is bounded by INT_MAX. (* mirror predicate (pointwise palindrome) implies list = rev list *)
Lemma mirror_to_rev : forall (lv : list Z),
  (forall k, 0 <= k < Zlength lv -> Znth k lv 0 = Znth (Zlength lv - 1 - k) lv 0) ->
  lv = rev lv.
Proof.
  intros lv Hmirror.
  rewrite (list_eq_ext lv (rev lv) 0).
  split.
  - rewrite !Zlength_correct. rewrite rev_length. reflexivity.
  - intros i Hi.
    rewrite Znth_rev_lem by exact Hi.
    apply Hmirror. exact Hi.
Qed.

Local Open Scope sac.

    Hilen : i < q_size_pre,

    pose proof (sum_sublist_step lv i ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as Hstep;
    pose proof (Hwifr (i + 1) ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as [Hlo Hhi];
    linarith
  end.
Qed.

From AUXLib Require Import int_auto Axioms Feq Idents ListLib VMap.

From SimpleC.EE.CAV.verify_20260607_152224_p072_will_it_fly Require Import p072_will_it_fly_goal.
Require Import Logic.LogicGenerator.demo932.Interface.

Import naive_C_Rules.
Require Import p072_will_it_fly.

(* Helper lemmas placed before Local Open Scope sac to avoid notation conflicts *)

(* fold_left with addition equals sum (fold_right) with accumulator *)
Lemma fold_left_Z_add_acc : forall (l : list Z) (acc : Z),
  fold_left (fun a x => a + x) l acc = acc + sum l.
Proof.
  induction l as [|a t IH]; intros acc.
  - simpl. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
  - simpl. rewrite IH. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* sum of sublist 0 (i+1) = sum of sublist 0 i + Znth i *)
Lemma sum_sublist_step : forall (lv : list Z) (i : Z),
  0 <= i < Zlength lv ->
  sum (sublist 0 (i + 1) lv) = sum (sublist 0 i lv) + Znth i lv 0.
Proof.
  intros lv i Hi.
  rewrite (sublist_split 0 (i + 1) i lv) by lia.
  rewrite (sublist_single 0 i lv) by lia.
  rewrite sum_app.
  unfold sum at 2. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* Znth of rev list equals Znth from the other end *)
Lemma Znth_rev_lem : forall (l : list Z) (k : Z),
  0 <= k < Zlength l ->
  Znth k (rev l) 0 = Znth (Zlength l - 1 - k) l 0.
Proof.
  intros l k Hk.
  unfold Znth.
  destruct (Z_lt_dec k 0). { lia. }
  destruct (Z_lt_dec (Zlength l - 1 - k) 0).
  { rewrite Zlength_correct in *. lia. }
  rewrite rev_nth by (rewrite Zlength_correct in Hk; lia).
  f_equal.
  rewrite Zlength_correct.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Nat2Z.id.
  simpl (Z.to_nat 1).
  lia.
Qed.

(* mirror predicate (pointwise palindrome) implies list = rev list *)
Lemma mirror_to_rev : forall (lv : list Z),
  (forall k, 0 <= k < Zlength lv -> Znth k lv 0 = Znth (Zlength lv - 1 - k) lv 0) ->
  lv = rev lv.
Proof.
  intros lv Hmirror.
  rewrite (list_eq_ext lv (rev lv) 0).
  split.
  - rewrite !Zlength_correct. rewrite rev_length. reflexivity.
  - intros i Hi.
    rewrite Znth_rev_lem by exact Hi.
    apply Hmirror. exact Hi.
Qed.

Local Open Scope sac.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_safety_wit_7 : p072_will_it_fly_safety_wit_7.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pures; dump_pre_spatial;
  match goal with
  | Hwifr : will_it_fly_int_range lv,
    Hilen : i < q_size_pre,
    Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
    Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv) |- _ =>
    pose proof (sum_sublist_step lv i ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as Hstep;
    pose proof (Hwifr (i + 1) ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as [Hlo Hhi];
    linarith
  end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_1 : p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures.
    all: dump_pre_spatial.
    all: try lia.
    all: try assumption.
    all: try (intros k [? ?]; lia).
    all: try (change (sublist 0 0 lv) with (@nil Z); unfold sum; simpl; lia).
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2 : p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures.
    all: dump_pre_spatial.
    all: try lia.
    all: try assumption.
    (* extended palindrome invariant *)
    + intros k [Hk1 Hk2].
      destruct (Z.eq_dec k i) as [Heq | Hne].
      * subst k. assumption.
      * match goal with
        | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < i) ->
              Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0 |- _ =>
          apply Hmirror; split; lia
        end.
    (* sum step *)
    + match goal with
      | Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv),
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
        Hilt : i < q_size_pre |- _ =>
        rewrite sum_sublist_step by (rewrite <- Hqlen; lia);
        linarith
      end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_1 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures; dump_pre_spatial.
    unfold problem_72_spec.
    assert (Heqi : i = q_size_pre) by lia.
    subst i.
    split. { right. reflexivity. }
    split.
    + intro _.
      split.
      * apply mirror_to_rev.
        intros k Hk.
        match goal with
        | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < q_size_pre) ->
              Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0,
          Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv |- _ =>
          apply Hmirror; rewrite Hqlen in Hk; split; lia
        end.
      * rewrite fold_left_Z_add_acc.
        match goal with
        | Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 q_size_pre lv),
          Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
          Hsle : s <= w_pre |- _ =>
          rewrite Hs, (sublist_self lv q_size_pre Hqlen); lia
        end.
    + intros _. reflexivity.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_2 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures; dump_pre_spatial.
    unfold problem_72_spec.
    assert (Heqi : i = q_size_pre) by lia.
    subst i.
    split. { left. reflexivity. }
    split.
    + intro H. discriminate H.
    + intro [_ Hfold].
      match goal with
      | Hgt : s > w_pre,
        Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 q_size_pre lv),
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv |- _ =>
        rewrite fold_left_Z_add_acc in Hfold;
        rewrite Hs, (sublist_self lv q_size_pre Hqlen) in Hgt;
        lia
      end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_3 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_3.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures; dump_pre_spatial.
    unfold problem_72_spec.
    split. { left. reflexivity. }
    split.
    + intro H. discriminate H.
    + intro [Hrev _].
      match goal with
      | Hneq : Znth i lv 0 <> Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - i) lv 0,
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
        Hilt : i < q_size_pre,
        Hige : 0 <= i |- _ =>
        apply Hneq;
        rewrite Hqlen;
        rewrite <- (Znth_rev_lem lv i ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia));
        rewrite <- Hrev;
        reflexivity
      end.
Qed.

Require Import Coq.Sorting.Permutation.
From AUXLib Require Import int_auto Axioms Feq Idents ListLib VMap.
Require Import SetsClass.SetsClass. Import SetsNotation.
From SimpleC.SL Require Import Mem SeparationLogic.
From SimpleC.EE.CAV.verify_20260607_152224_p072_will_it_fly Require Import p072_will_it_fly_goal.
Require Import Logic.LogicGenerator.demo932.Interface.
Local Open Scope Z_scope.
Local Open Scope sets.
Local Open Scope string_scope.
Local Open Scope list.
Import naive_C_Rules.
Require Import p072_will_it_fly.

(* Helper lemmas placed before Local Open Scope sac to avoid notation conflicts *)

(* fold_left with addition equals sum (fold_right) with accumulator *)
Lemma fold_left_Z_add_acc : forall (l : list Z) (acc : Z),
  fold_left (fun a x => a + x) l acc = acc + sum l.
Proof.
  induction l as [|a t IH]; intros acc.
  - simpl. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
  - simpl. rewrite IH. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* sum of sublist 0 (i+1) = sum of sublist 0 i + Znth i *)
Lemma sum_sublist_step : forall (lv : list Z) (i : Z),
  0 <= i < Zlength lv ->
  sum (sublist 0 (i + 1) lv) = sum (sublist 0 i lv) + Znth i lv 0.
Proof.
  intros lv i Hi.
  rewrite (sublist_split 0 (i + 1) i lv) by lia.
  rewrite (sublist_single 0 i lv) by lia.
  rewrite sum_app.
  unfold sum at 2. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* Znth of rev list equals Znth from the other end *)
Lemma Znth_rev_lem : forall (l : list Z) (k : Z),
  0 <= k < Zlength l ->
  Znth k (rev l) 0 = Znth (Zlength l - 1 - k) l 0.
Proof.
  intros l k Hk.
  unfold Znth.
  destruct (Z_lt_dec k 0). { lia. }
  destruct (Z_lt_dec (Zlength l - 1 - k) 0).
  { rewrite Zlength_correct in *. lia. }
  rewrite rev_nth by (rewrite Zlength_correct in Hk; lia).
  f_equal.
  rewrite Zlength_correct.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Nat2Z.id.
  simpl (Z.to_nat 1).
  lia.
Qed.

(* mirror predicate (pointwise palindrome) implies list = rev list *)
Lemma mirror_to_rev : forall (lv : list Z),
  (forall k, 0 <= k < Zlength lv -> Znth k lv 0 = Znth (Zlength lv - 1 - k) lv 0) ->
  lv = rev lv.
Proof.
  intros lv Hmirror.
  rewrite (list_eq_ext lv (rev lv) 0).
  split.
  - rewrite !Zlength_correct. rewrite length_rev. reflexivity.
  - intros i Hi.
    rewrite Znth_rev_lem by exact Hi.
    apply Hmirror. exact Hi.
Qed.

Local Open Scope sac.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_safety_wit_7 : p072_will_it_fly_safety_wit_7.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pures; dump_pre_spatial;
  match goal with
  | Hwifr : will_it_fly_int_range lv,
    Hilen : i < q_size_pre,
    Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
    Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv) |- _ =>
    pose proof (sum_sublist_step lv i ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as Hstep;
    pose proof (Hwifr (i + 1) ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as [Hlo Hhi];
    linarith
  end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_1 : p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures.
    all: dump_pre_spatial.
    all: try lia.
    all: try assumption.
    all: try (intros k [? ?]; lia).
    all: try (change (sublist 0 0 lv) with (@nil Z); unfold sum; simpl; lia).
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2 : p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures.
    all: dump_pre_spatial.
    all: try lia.
    all: try assumption.
    (* extended palindrome invariant *)
    + intros k [Hk1 Hk2].
      destruct (Z.eq_dec k i) as [Heq | Hne].
      * subst k. assumption.
      * match goal with
        | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < i) ->
              Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0 |- _ =>
          apply Hmirror; split; lia
        end.
    (* sum step *)
    + match goal with
      | Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv),
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
        Hilt : i < q_size_pre |- _ =>
        rewrite sum_sublist_step by (rewrite <- Hqlen; lia);
        linarith
      end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_1 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures; dump_pre_spatial.
    unfold problem_72_spec.
    assert (Heqi : i = q_size_pre) by lia.
    subst i.
    split. { right. reflexivity. }
    split.
    + intro _.
      split.
      * apply mirror_to_rev.
        intros k Hk.
        match goal with
        | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < q_size_pre) ->
              Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0,
          Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv |- _ =>
          apply Hmirror; rewrite Hqlen in Hk; split; lia
        end.
      * rewrite fold_left_Z_add_acc.
        match goal with
        | Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 q_size_pre lv),
          Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
          Hsle : s <= w_pre |- _ =>
          rewrite Hs, (sublist_self lv q_size_pre Hqlen); lia
        end.
    + intros _. reflexivity.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_2 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures; dump_pre_spatial.
    unfold problem_72_spec.
    assert (Heqi : i = q_size_pre) by lia.
    subst i.
    split. { left. reflexivity. }
    split.
    + intro H. discriminate H.
    + intro [_ Hfold].
      match goal with
      | Hgt : s > w_pre,
        Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 q_size_pre lv),
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv |- _ =>
        rewrite fold_left_Z_add_acc in Hfold;
        rewrite Hs, (sublist_self lv q_size_pre Hqlen) in Hgt;
        lia
      end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_3 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_3.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures; dump_pre_spatial.
    unfold problem_72_spec.
    split. { left. reflexivity. }
    split.
    + intro H. discriminate H.
    + intro [Hrev _].
      match goal with
      | Hneq : Znth i lv 0 <> Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - i) lv 0,
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
        Hilt : i < q_size_pre,
        Hige : 0 <= i |- _ =>
        apply Hneq;
        rewrite Hqlen;
        rewrite <- (Znth_rev_lem lv i ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia));
        rewrite <- Hrev;
        reflexivity
      end.
Qed.

content.thinking:
The error is `No such goal` at line 99, characters 2-21, which is `split_pure_spatial`. This means that after `pre_process`, the goal can't be split into pure and spatial parts by `split_pure_spatial`.

Looking at the entail_wit_1 VC:
```
" (0 <= q_size_pre) " && " (q_size_pre < INT_MAX) " && " (q_size_pre = (Zlength (lv))) " && " (problem_72_pre lv w_pre) " && " (will_it_fly_int_range lv) " && (IntArray.full q_pre q_size_pre lv) 
```

After `pre_process`, the pure hypotheses get extracted and the goal should already be decomposed into pure and spatial components, but it seems the goal isn't matching what `split_pure_spatial` expects.

The "No such goal" error likely means `pre_process` with `try (solve [entailer!])` actually closed the goal entirely for `entail_wit_1`. If `entailer!` successfully proved the entailment, there would be no goal left for `split_pure_spatial` to operate on. So the error message about `split_pure_spatial` failing with `No such goal` actually suggests that `pre_process` successfully closed the goal entirely, which would mean `entailer!` was powerful enough to handle both the vacuous forall and the sum unfolding. Let me just try the simple approach with `pre_process.` followed by `Qed` and see if it works.

Require Import Coq.ZArith.ZArith.
Require Import Coq.Bool.Bool.
Require Import Coq.Strings.String.
Require Import Coq.Strings.Ascii.
Require Import Coq.Lists.List.
Require Import Coq.Classes.RelationClasses.
Require Import Coq.Classes.Morphisms.
Require Import Coq.micromega.Psatz.
Require Import Coq.Sorting.Permutation.
From AUXLib Require Import int_auto Axioms Feq Idents ListLib VMap.
Require Import SetsClass.SetsClass. Import SetsNotation.
From SimpleC.SL Require Import Mem SeparationLogic.
From SimpleC.EE.CAV.verify_20260607_152224_p072_will_it_fly Require Import p072_will_it_fly_goal.
Require Import Logic.LogicGenerator.demo932.Interface.
Local Open Scope Z_scope.
Local Open Scope sets.
Local Open Scope string_scope.
Local Open Scope list.
Import naive_C_Rules.
Require Import p072_will_it_fly.

(* Helper lemmas placed before Local Open Scope sac to avoid notation conflicts *)

(* fold_left with addition equals sum (fold_right) with accumulator *)
Lemma fold_left_Z_add_acc : forall (l : list Z) (acc : Z),
  fold_left (fun a x => a + x) l acc = acc + sum l.
Proof.
  induction l as [|a t IH]; intros acc.
  - simpl. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
  - simpl. rewrite IH. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* sum of sublist 0 (i+1) = sum of sublist 0 i + Znth i *)
Lemma sum_sublist_step : forall (lv : list Z) (i : Z),
  0 <= i < Zlength lv ->
  sum (sublist 0 (i + 1) lv) = sum (sublist 0 i lv) + Znth i lv 0.
Proof.
  intros lv i Hi.
  rewrite (sublist_split 0 (i + 1) i lv) by lia.
  rewrite (sublist_single 0 i lv) by lia.
  rewrite sum_app.
  unfold sum at 2. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* Znth of rev list equals Znth from the other end *)
Lemma Znth_rev_lem : forall (l : list Z) (k : Z),
  0 <= k < Zlength l ->
  Znth k (rev l) 0 = Znth (Zlength l - 1 - k) l 0.
Proof.
  intros l k Hk.
  unfold Znth.
  destruct (Z_lt_dec k 0). { lia. }
  destruct (Z_lt_dec (Zlength l - 1 - k) 0).
  { rewrite Zlength_correct in *. lia. }
  rewrite rev_nth by (rewrite Zlength_correct in Hk; lia).
  f_equal.
  rewrite Zlength_correct.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Nat2Z.id.
  simpl (Z.to_nat 1).
  lia.
Qed.

(* mirror predicate (pointwise palindrome) implies list = rev list *)
Lemma mirror_to_rev : forall (lv : list Z),
  (forall k, 0 <= k < Zlength lv -> Znth k lv 0 = Znth (Zlength lv - 1 - k) lv 0) ->
  lv = rev lv.
Proof.
  intros lv Hmirror.
  rewrite (list_eq_ext lv (rev lv) 0).
  split.
  - rewrite !Zlength_correct. rewrite length_rev. reflexivity.
  - intros i Hi.
    rewrite Znth_rev_lem by exact Hi.
    apply Hmirror. exact Hi.
Qed.

Local Open Scope sac.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_safety_wit_7 : p072_will_it_fly_safety_wit_7.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pures; dump_pre_spatial;
  match goal with
  | Hwifr : will_it_fly_int_range lv,
    Hilen : i < q_size_pre,
    Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
    Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv) |- _ =>
    pose proof (sum_sublist_step lv i ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as Hstep;
    pose proof (Hwifr (i + 1) ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as [Hlo Hhi];
    lia
  end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_1 : p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2 : p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures.
    all: dump_pre_spatial.
    all: try lia.
    all: try assumption.
    (* extended palindrome invariant *)
    + intros k [Hk1 Hk2].
      destruct (Z.eq_dec k i) as [Heq | Hne].
      * subst k. assumption.
      * match goal with
        | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < i) ->
              Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0 |- _ =>
          apply Hmirror; split; lia
        end.
    (* sum step *)
    + match goal with
      | Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv),
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
        Hilt : i < q_size_pre |- _ =>
        rewrite sum_sublist_step by (rewrite <- Hqlen; lia);
        linarith
      end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_1 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures; dump_pre_spatial.
    unfold problem_72_spec.
    assert (Heqi : i = q_size_pre) by lia.
    subst i.
    split. { right. reflexivity. }
    split.
    + intro _.
      split.
      * apply mirror_to_rev.
        intros k Hk.
        match goal with
        | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < q_size_pre) ->
              Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0,
          Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv |- _ =>
          apply Hmirror; rewrite Hqlen in Hk; split; lia
        end.
      * rewrite fold_left_Z_add_acc.
        match goal with
        | Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 q_size_pre lv),
          Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
          Hsle : s <= w_pre |- _ =>
          rewrite Hs, (sublist_self lv q_size_pre Hqlen); lia
        end.
    + intros _. reflexivity.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_2 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures; dump_pre_spatial.
    unfold problem_72_spec.
    assert (Heqi : i = q_size_pre) by lia.
    subst i.
    split. { left. reflexivity. }
    split.
    + intro H. discriminate H.
    + intro [_ Hfold].
      match goal with
      | Hgt : s > w_pre,
        Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 q_size_pre lv),
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv |- _ =>
        rewrite fold_left_Z_add_acc in Hfold;
        rewrite Hs, (sublist_self lv q_size_pre Hqlen) in Hgt;
        lia
      end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_3 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_3.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures; dump_pre_spatial.
    unfold problem_72_spec.
    split. { left. reflexivity. }
    split.
    + intro H. discriminate H.
    + intro [Hrev _].
      match goal with
      | Hneq : Znth i lv 0 <> Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - i) lv 0,
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
        Hilt : i < q_size_pre,
        Hige : 0 <= i |- _ =>
        apply Hneq;
        rewrite Hqlen;
        rewrite <- (Znth_rev_lem lv i ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia));
        rewrite <- Hrev;
        reflexivity
      end.
Qed.

content.thinking:
The syntax error is at line 140, `+ intro _.`. This is inside a `split_pures; dump_pre_spatial.` which generates only ONE goal (since the conclusion has one pure fact `problem_72_spec lv w_pre 1`). So after `split_pures; dump_pre_spatial`, there's ONE goal and the `+` bullet syntax doesn't apply here - I should not use bullets inside.

Wait, but `split_pures` should generate one goal for the single pure fact. Then after the `split_pures; dump_pre_spatial.`, there should be one goal. 

I see the issue now - the period after `dump_pre_spatial` closes the tactic block, so the `unfold` and subsequent tactics that follow are in a separate tactic block. That's the problem. I need to chain these tactics together without the period, or structure them differently so the `unfold`, `split`, and bullet-based goals all happen within the same tactic context. After `split_pure_spatial`, the spatial goal becomes `pure_preconditions && IntArray.full |-- IntArray.full q_pre q_size_pre lv`, which the `cancel.` tactic should handle, while the pure goal extracts the `problem_72_spec` constraint that needs to be verified separately. has all the pure hypotheses plus `IntArray.full` in the context. I'm trying to understand whether `dump_pre_spatial` removes the spatial part from the context or just transforms the goal. Looking at how `dump_spatial_left` works—it says `Q -> P |-- "Q"`, meaning to prove `P |-- "Q"`, I need to prove `Q` with `P` available as a hypothesis. After applying `dump_pre_spatial`, I'm proving `Q` as a regular Prop in Coq, but the spatial hypothesis `P` should still be accessible in the context.

Actually, the tactic just applies `dump_spatial_left`, which creates a new goal of `Q` while preserving all the previously introduced hypotheses from the original context. So the pure propositions extracted from the left side of the entailment during preprocessing remain available.

After `pre_process` runs, those pure hypotheses get introduced into the Coq context via `intros`, leaving the spatial predicates like `IntArray.full` as part of the separation logic entailment. The goal then shows the pure hypotheses as context entries and the remaining spatial part as the entailment to prove. Now `split_pure_spatial` needs to handle splitting the pure and spatial components of the entailment... The issue is that I'm trying to use a `+` bullet inside a `-` focused block, but Coq's tactic language doesn't allow lower-priority bullets nested within higher-priority ones—after `-`, I need to use `*` or numbered bullets instead, or restructure to avoid nested bullets altogether.

content.text: The issue is nested `-` and `+` bullets. I need to restructure to avoid this. Let me rewrite the return witnesses:

The fix is to either:
1. Use `*` (star) bullets inside `-` blocks
2. Use `{ }` braces for nesting
3. Avoid using bullets inside the `-` block and use `all:` or sequential tactics
Actually, looking at the error more carefully—it's pointing to line 140 where I have `+ intro _.` at the same indentation level as the rest of the `-` block. In Coq, `+` should work inside a `-` focus, but maybe the issue is something else with how the bullets are being parsed.

I'm wondering if the real issue is that `split_pures; dump_pre_spatial.` with the period at the end might be closing the entire `-` focus prematurely, rather than keeping it open for the subsequent `+` bullets to handle the subgoals created by `split.` So `dump_pre_spatial` transforms each goal `P |-- "Q_i"` into just `Q_i`, leaving N open goals in the `-` focus. (* Helper lemmas placed before Local Open Scope sac to avoid notation conflicts *)

(* fold_left with addition equals sum (fold_right) with accumulator *)
Lemma fold_left_Z_add_acc : forall (l : list Z) (acc : Z),
  fold_left (fun a x => a + x) l acc = acc + sum l.
Proof.
  induction l as [|a t IH]; intros acc.
  - simpl. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
  - simpl. rewrite IH. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* sum of sublist 0 (i+1) = sum of sublist 0 i + Znth i *)
Lemma sum_sublist_step : forall (lv : list Z) (i : Z),
  0 <= i < Zlength lv ->
  sum (sublist 0 (i + 1) lv) = sum (sublist 0 i lv) + Znth i lv 0.
Proof.
  intros lv i Hi.
  rewrite (sublist_split 0 (i + 1) i lv) by lia.
  rewrite (sublist_single 0 i lv) by lia.
  rewrite sum_app.
  unfold sum at 2. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* Znth of rev list equals Znth from the other end *)
Lemma Znth_rev_lem : forall (l : list Z) (k : Z),
  0 <= k < Zlength l ->
  Znth k (rev l) 0 = Znth (Zlength l - 1 - k) l 0.
Proof.
  intros l k Hk.
  unfold Znth.
  destruct (Z_lt_dec k 0). { lia. }
  destruct (Z_lt_dec (Zlength l - 1 - k) 0).
  { rewrite Zlength_correct in *. lia. }
  rewrite rev_nth by (rewrite Zlength_correct in Hk; lia).
  f_equal.
  rewrite Zlength_correct.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Nat2Z.id.
  simpl (Z.to_nat 1).
  lia.
Qed.

(* mirror predicate (pointwise palindrome) implies list = rev list *)
Lemma mirror_to_rev : forall (lv : list Z),
  (forall k, 0 <= k < Zlength lv -> Znth k lv 0 = Znth (Zlength lv - 1 - k) lv 0) ->
  lv = rev lv.
Proof.
  intros lv Hmirror.
  rewrite (list_eq_ext lv (rev lv) 0).
  split.
  - rewrite !Zlength_correct. rewrite length_rev. reflexivity.
  - intros i Hi.
    rewrite Znth_rev_lem by exact Hi.
    apply Hmirror. exact Hi.
Qed.

    Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv) |- _ =>
    pose proof (sum_sublist_step lv i ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as Hstep;
    pose proof (Hwifr (i + 1) ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as [Hlo Hhi];

  end.
Qed.

content.thinking:
For `entail_wit_2`, the structure is:
- `split_pure_spatial` gives 2 goals
- `-` block for spatial: `cancel`
- `-` block for pure: `split_pures` gives multiple goals
  - `all: dump_pre_spatial; try lia; try assumption` handles most
  - `+` bullets for the forall and sum goals inside `-`

This is the same nesting problem. Let me use the `entailer!` approach or use `all:` with different handling.

Actually, maybe I should try: after `split_pures`, use `all:` with a broad tactic that handles everything, including the forall and sum goals. This avoids bullets altogether.
For the forall goal, I need to introduce the variables and hypothesis, then use decidability of equality on integers to split into the case where the index matches and where it doesn't. For the sum goal, I'll rewrite using the step lemma and then apply arithmetic reasoning. I can wrap these in `try` tactics and apply them across all remaining goals with `all:`.

There's an important detail here: after substituting the index variable, the goal becomes an equality that should already exist as a hypothesis from the verification condition. The VC includes `Znth i lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - i) lv 0` in the context, so `assumption` should close that branch directly.

However, I need to be careful with the proof structure—I can't rely on `all: try assumption` to handle the forall goal automatically since the introduction and case analysis need to happen first. Let me restructure the `entail_wit_2` proof to avoid nested bullets and handle each case cleanly.

(* Helper lemmas placed before Local Open Scope sac to avoid notation conflicts *)

(* fold_left with addition equals sum (fold_right) with accumulator *)
Lemma fold_left_Z_add_acc : forall (l : list Z) (acc : Z),
  fold_left (fun a x => a + x) l acc = acc + sum l.
Proof.
  induction l as [|a t IH]; intros acc.
  - simpl. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
  - simpl. rewrite IH. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* sum of sublist 0 (i+1) = sum of sublist 0 i + Znth i *)
Lemma sum_sublist_step : forall (lv : list Z) (i : Z),
  0 <= i < Zlength lv ->
  sum (sublist 0 (i + 1) lv) = sum (sublist 0 i lv) + Znth i lv 0.
Proof.
  intros lv i Hi.
  rewrite (sublist_split 0 (i + 1) i lv) by lia.
  rewrite (sublist_single 0 i lv) by lia.
  rewrite sum_app.
  unfold sum at 2. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* Znth of rev list equals Znth from the other end *)
Lemma Znth_rev_lem : forall (l : list Z) (k : Z),
  0 <= k < Zlength l ->
  Znth k (rev l) 0 = Znth (Zlength l - 1 - k) l 0.
Proof.
  intros l k Hk.
  unfold Znth.
  destruct (Z_lt_dec k 0). { lia. }
  destruct (Z_lt_dec (Zlength l - 1 - k) 0).
  { rewrite Zlength_correct in *. lia. }
  rewrite rev_nth by (rewrite Zlength_correct in Hk; lia).
  f_equal.
  rewrite Zlength_correct.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Nat2Z.id.
  simpl (Z.to_nat 1).
  lia.
Qed.

(* mirror predicate (pointwise palindrome) implies list = rev list *)
Lemma mirror_to_rev : forall (lv : list Z),
  (forall k, 0 <= k < Zlength lv -> Znth k lv 0 = Znth (Zlength lv - 1 - k) lv 0) ->
  lv = rev lv.
Proof.
  intros lv Hmirror.
  rewrite (list_eq_ext lv (rev lv) 0).
  split.
  - rewrite !Zlength_correct. rewrite length_rev. reflexivity.
  - intros i Hi.
    rewrite Znth_rev_lem by exact Hi.
    apply Hmirror. exact Hi.
Qed.

content.thinking:
The `safety_wit_7` VC has the conclusion:
```
" ((s + (Znth i lv 0) ) <= INT_MAX) " && " ((INT_MIN) <= (s + (Znth i lv 0) )) "
```

After `split_pures`, there are 2 goals. After `dump_pre_spatial`, each of these goals becomes a pure Prop. Then the `match goal` applies to each.

The `split_pures; dump_pre_spatial;` (with `;` not `.`) means: apply `split_pures`, then for each generated subgoal, apply `dump_pre_spatial`, then for each goal generated by that, apply the `match goal` tactic.
I'm realizing that in Coq's tactic language, the semicolon operator chains tactics so that each subsequent tactic applies to all subgoals produced by the previous one. So `split_pures; dump_pre_spatial; match goal { ... }` first splits the goal into 2 subgoals, then transforms each with `dump_pre_spatial` to get pure Prop goals, and finally applies the pattern matching to handle each result.

The `match goal` block then uses `pose proof` to introduce the step hypothesis and the bounds from `Hwifr`, giving me the sum equality along with the lower and upper bounds needed for `lia` to discharge both goals. Let me check if the `split_pures` tactic is correctly handling the conjunction of safety conditions in the verification condition, since the LHS should contain both spatial and pure facts after preprocessing. I'm concerned that `split_pures` might create multiple subgoals, and then applying `match goal` with a `pose proof` statement could cause naming conflicts when Coq tries to introduce `Hstep` in both branches. I need to think through whether Coq handles this gracefully or if I need a different approach to avoid shadowing issues.

Actually, each subgoal maintains its own independent context after `split_pures`, so the subsequent tactic application works on each one separately—naming `Hstep` in both contexts should be fine. Let me just test what I have so far.

content.thinking:
Line 108 has `entailer!` in the `entail_wit_2` proof. `No product even after head-reduction` means `entailer!` encountered a goal that doesn't match its expected form. This happens when the goal is not an entailment of the form `P |-- Q`.

After `pre_process`, the goal might have already been modified. If `pre_process` successfully handles most goals but leaves some residual, `entailer!` might fail on those.

Let me look at what `entailer!` does: `set_String_name; try poly_store_unfold; Rename entailer_pure; simpl_entail; subst_all_strings`. 

The issue is that `Rename entailer_pure` and `simpl_entail` expect separation logic entailments, not pure Prop goals. So if the goal has already been reduced to a pure proposition, `entailer!` will fail. Since `pre_process` calls `try (solve [entailer!])` and then I call `entailer!` again explicitly, I might be applying it to a goal that's no longer in the right form.

The error "No product even after head-reduction" suggests `entailer!` is being applied to something that isn't actually an entailment. For `entail_wit_2`, I should try using `split_pure_spatial` to separate the pure and spatial parts, then handle the forall and sum goals with targeted tactics instead of nested bullets. Building the tactic chain... Finishing the tactic chain to complete the proof step, then realizing the key insight: chaining tactics with semicolons applies them to all subgoals generated by `split_pures`, and wrapping with `try` prevents failures without needing bullets. Now I'm reconsidering whether this approach works correctly for `safety_wit_7`—the `split_pures; dump_pre_spatial; match goal` chain should apply the match to both pure Prop goals and pose the hypotheses in each context independently, which should be fine. But I need to verify what happens after `split_pures` generates the two goals for the bounds checks.

Now I'm moving on to rewrite `entail_wit_2` and double-check `entail_wit_1`.

content.text: The `entailer!` tactic fails on certain goal forms. Let me use the `split_pure_spatial` approach with chained `;` tactics to avoid bullet nesting:

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2 : p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* extended palindrome invariant forall k, 0<=k<i+1 -> ... *)
  - intros k [Hk1 Hk2].
    destruct (Z.eq_dec k i) as [Heq | Hne].
    + subst k. assumption.
    + match goal with
      | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < i) ->
            Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0 |- _ =>
        apply Hmirror; split; lia
      end.
  (* sum step: s + Znth i lv 0 = sum (sublist 0 (i+1) lv) *)
  - match goal with
    | Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv),
      Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
      Hilt : i < q_size_pre |- _ =>
      rewrite sum_sublist_step by (rewrite <- Hqlen; lia);
      lia
    end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2 : p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures;
    dump_pre_spatial;
    try lia;
    try assumption;
    try (intros k [Hk1 Hk2];
         destruct (Z.eq_dec k i) as [Heq | Hne];
         [ subst k; assumption
         | match goal with
           | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < i) ->
                 Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0 |- _ =>
             apply Hmirror; split; lia
           end ]);
    try (match goal with
         | Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv),
           Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
           Hilt : i < q_size_pre |- _ =>
           rewrite sum_sublist_step by (rewrite <- Hqlen; lia); lia
         end).
Qed.

Require Import Coq.ZArith.ZArith.
Require Import Coq.Bool.Bool.
Require Import Coq.Strings.String.
Require Import Coq.Strings.Ascii.
Require Import Coq.Lists.List.
Require Import Coq.Classes.RelationClasses.
Require Import Coq.Classes.Morphisms.
Require Import Coq.micromega.Psatz.
Require Import Coq.Sorting.Permutation.
From AUXLib Require Import int_auto Axioms Feq Idents ListLib VMap.
Require Import SetsClass.SetsClass. Import SetsNotation.
From SimpleC.SL Require Import Mem SeparationLogic.
From SimpleC.EE.CAV.verify_20260607_152224_p072_will_it_fly Require Import p072_will_it_fly_goal.
Require Import Logic.LogicGenerator.demo932.Interface.
Local Open Scope Z_scope.
Local Open Scope sets.
Local Open Scope string_scope.
Local Open Scope list.
Import naive_C_Rules.
Require Import p072_will_it_fly.

(* Helper lemmas placed before Local Open Scope sac to avoid notation conflicts *)

(* fold_left with addition equals sum (fold_right) with accumulator *)
Lemma fold_left_Z_add_acc : forall (l : list Z) (acc : Z),
  fold_left (fun a x => a + x) l acc = acc + sum l.
Proof.
  induction l as [|a t IH]; intros acc.
  - simpl. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
  - simpl. rewrite IH. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* sum of sublist 0 (i+1) = sum of sublist 0 i + Znth i *)
Lemma sum_sublist_step : forall (lv : list Z) (i : Z),
  0 <= i < Zlength lv ->
  sum (sublist 0 (i + 1) lv) = sum (sublist 0 i lv) + Znth i lv 0.
Proof.
  intros lv i Hi.
  rewrite (sublist_split 0 (i + 1) i lv) by lia.
  rewrite (sublist_single 0 i lv) by lia.
  rewrite sum_app.
  unfold sum at 2. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* Znth of rev list equals Znth from the other end *)
Lemma Znth_rev_lem : forall (l : list Z) (k : Z),
  0 <= k < Zlength l ->
  Znth k (rev l) 0 = Znth (Zlength l - 1 - k) l 0.
Proof.
  intros l k Hk.
  unfold Znth.
  destruct (Z_lt_dec k 0). { lia. }
  destruct (Z_lt_dec (Zlength l - 1 - k) 0).
  { rewrite Zlength_correct in *. lia. }
  rewrite rev_nth by (rewrite Zlength_correct in Hk; lia).
  f_equal.
  rewrite Zlength_correct.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Nat2Z.id.
  simpl (Z.to_nat 1).
  lia.
Qed.

(* mirror predicate (pointwise palindrome) implies list = rev list *)
Lemma mirror_to_rev : forall (lv : list Z),
  (forall k, 0 <= k < Zlength lv -> Znth k lv 0 = Znth (Zlength lv - 1 - k) lv 0) ->
  lv = rev lv.
Proof.
  intros lv Hmirror.
  rewrite (list_eq_ext lv (rev lv) 0).
  split.
  - rewrite !Zlength_correct. rewrite length_rev. reflexivity.
  - intros i Hi.
    rewrite Znth_rev_lem by exact Hi.
    apply Hmirror. exact Hi.
Qed.

Local Open Scope sac.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_safety_wit_7 : p072_will_it_fly_safety_wit_7.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pures; dump_pre_spatial;
  match goal with
  | Hwifr : will_it_fly_int_range lv,
    Hilen : i < q_size_pre,
    Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
    Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv) |- _ =>
    pose proof (sum_sublist_step lv i ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as Hstep;
    pose proof (Hwifr (i + 1) ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as [Hlo Hhi];
    lia
  end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_1 : p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2 : p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* extended palindrome invariant forall k, 0<=k<i+1 -> ... *)
  - intros k [Hk1 Hk2].
    destruct (Z.eq_dec k i) as [Heq | Hne].
    + subst k. assumption.
    + match goal with
      | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < i) ->
            Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0 |- _ =>
        apply Hmirror; split; lia
      end.
  (* sum step: s + Znth i lv 0 = sum (sublist 0 (i+1) lv) *)
  - match goal with
    | Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv),
      Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
      Hilt : i < q_size_pre |- _ =>
      rewrite sum_sublist_step by (rewrite <- Hqlen; lia);
      lia
    end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_1 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  assert (Heqi : i = q_size_pre) by lia.
  subst i.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* remaining: problem_72_spec lv w_pre 1 *)
  unfold problem_72_spec.
  split. { right. reflexivity. }
  split.
  - intro _.
    split.
    + apply mirror_to_rev.
      intros k Hk.
      match goal with
      | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < q_size_pre) ->
            Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0,
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv |- _ =>
        apply Hmirror; rewrite Hqlen in Hk; split; lia
      end.
    + rewrite fold_left_Z_add_acc.
      match goal with
      | Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 q_size_pre lv),
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
        Hsle : s <= w_pre |- _ =>
        rewrite Hs, (sublist_self lv q_size_pre Hqlen); lia
      end.
  - intros _. reflexivity.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_2 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  assert (Heqi : i = q_size_pre) by lia.
  subst i.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* remaining: problem_72_spec lv w_pre 0 *)
  unfold problem_72_spec.
  split. { left. reflexivity. }
  split.
  - intro H. discriminate H.
  - intro [_ Hfold].
    match goal with
    | Hgt : s > w_pre,
      Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 q_size_pre lv),
      Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv |- _ =>
      rewrite fold_left_Z_add_acc in Hfold;
      rewrite Hs, (sublist_self lv q_size_pre Hqlen) in Hgt;
      lia
    end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_3 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_3.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* remaining: problem_72_spec lv w_pre 0 *)
  unfold problem_72_spec.
  split. { left. reflexivity. }
  split.
  - intro H. discriminate H.
  - intro [Hrev _].
    match goal with
    | Hneq : Znth i lv 0 <> Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - i) lv 0,
      Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
      Hilt : i < q_size_pre,
      Hige : 0 <= i |- _ =>
      apply Hneq;
      rewrite Hqlen;
      rewrite <- (Znth_rev_lem lv i ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia));
      rewrite <- Hrev;
      reflexivity
    end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_1 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  assert (Heqi : i = q_size_pre) by lia.
  subst i.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* remaining: problem_72_spec lv w_pre 1 *)
  unfold problem_72_spec.
  split. { right. reflexivity. }
  split.
  - intro _.
    split.
    + apply mirror_to_rev.
      intros k Hk.
      match goal with
      | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < q_size_pre) ->
            Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0,
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv |- _ =>
        apply Hmirror; rewrite Hqlen in Hk; split; lia
      end.
    + rewrite fold_left_Z_add_acc.
      match goal with
      | Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 q_size_pre lv),
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
        Hsle : s <= w_pre |- _ =>
        rewrite Hs, (sublist_self lv q_size_pre Hqlen); lia
      end.
  - intros _. reflexivity.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_2 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  assert (Heqi : i = q_size_pre) by lia.
  subst i.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* remaining: problem_72_spec lv w_pre 0 *)
  unfold problem_72_spec.
  split. { left. reflexivity. }
  split.
  - intro H. discriminate H.
  - intro [_ Hfold].
    match goal with
    | Hgt : s > w_pre,
      Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 q_size_pre lv),
      Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv |- _ =>
      rewrite fold_left_Z_add_acc in Hfold;
      rewrite Hs, (sublist_self lv q_size_pre Hqlen) in Hgt;
      lia
    end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_3 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_3.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* remaining: problem_72_spec lv w_pre 0 *)
  unfold problem_72_spec.
  split. { left. reflexivity. }
  split.
  - intro H. discriminate H.
  - intro [Hrev _].
    match goal with
    | Hneq : Znth i lv 0 <> Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - i) lv 0,
      Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,

From SimpleC.EE.CAV.verify_20260607_152224_p072_will_it_fly Require Import p072_will_it_fly_goal.
Require Import Logic.LogicGenerator.demo932.Interface.
Local Open Scope Z_scope.
Local Open Scope sets.
Local Open Scope string_scope.
Local Open Scope list.
Import naive_C_Rules.
Require Import p072_will_it_fly.

(* Helper lemmas placed before Local Open Scope sac to avoid notation conflicts *)

(* fold_left with addition equals sum (fold_right) with accumulator *)
Lemma fold_left_Z_add_acc : forall (l : list Z) (acc : Z),
  fold_left (fun a x => a + x) l acc = acc + sum l.
Proof.
  induction l as [|a t IH]; intros acc.
  - simpl. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
  - simpl. rewrite IH. unfold sum. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* sum of sublist 0 (i+1) = sum of sublist 0 i + Znth i *)
Lemma sum_sublist_step : forall (lv : list Z) (i : Z),
  0 <= i < Zlength lv ->
  sum (sublist 0 (i + 1) lv) = sum (sublist 0 i lv) + Znth i lv 0.
Proof.
  intros lv i Hi.
  rewrite (sublist_split 0 (i + 1) i lv) by lia.
  rewrite (sublist_single 0 i lv) by lia.
  rewrite sum_app.
  unfold sum at 2. simpl. ring.
Qed.

(* Znth of rev list equals Znth from the other end *)
Lemma Znth_rev_lem : forall (l : list Z) (k : Z),
  0 <= k < Zlength l ->
  Znth k (rev l) 0 = Znth (Zlength l - 1 - k) l 0.
Proof.
  intros l k Hk.
  unfold Znth.
  destruct (Z_lt_dec k 0). { lia. }
  destruct (Z_lt_dec (Zlength l - 1 - k) 0).
  { rewrite Zlength_correct in *. lia. }
  rewrite rev_nth by (rewrite Zlength_correct in Hk; lia).
  f_equal.
  rewrite Zlength_correct.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Z2Nat.inj_sub by lia.
  rewrite Nat2Z.id.
  simpl (Z.to_nat 1).
  lia.
Qed.

(* mirror predicate (pointwise palindrome) implies list = rev list *)
Lemma mirror_to_rev : forall (lv : list Z),
  (forall k, 0 <= k < Zlength lv -> Znth k lv 0 = Znth (Zlength lv - 1 - k) lv 0) ->
  lv = rev lv.
Proof.
  intros lv Hmirror.
  rewrite (list_eq_ext lv (rev lv) 0).
  split.
  - rewrite !Zlength_correct. rewrite length_rev. reflexivity.
  - intros i Hi.
    rewrite Znth_rev_lem by exact Hi.
    apply Hmirror. exact Hi.
Qed.

Local Open Scope sac.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_safety_wit_7 : p072_will_it_fly_safety_wit_7.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pures; dump_pre_spatial;
  match goal with
  | Hwifr : will_it_fly_int_range lv,
    Hilen : i < q_size_pre,
    Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
    Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv) |- _ =>
    pose proof (sum_sublist_step lv i ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as Hstep;
    pose proof (Hwifr (i + 1) ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia)) as [Hlo Hhi];
    lia
  end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_1 : p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2 : p072_will_it_fly_entail_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  split_pure_spatial.
  - cancel.
  - split_pures;
    dump_pre_spatial;
    try lia;
    try assumption;
    try (intros k [Hk1 Hk2];
         destruct (Z.eq_dec k i) as [Heq | Hne];
         [ subst k; assumption
         | match goal with
           | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < i) ->
                 Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0 |- _ =>
             apply Hmirror; split; lia
           end ]);
    try (match goal with
         | Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 i lv),
           Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
           Hilt : i < q_size_pre |- _ =>
           rewrite sum_sublist_step by (rewrite <- Hqlen; lia); lia
         end).
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_1 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  assert (Heqi : i = q_size_pre) by lia.
  subst i.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* remaining: problem_72_spec lv w_pre 1 *)
  unfold problem_72_spec.
  split. { right. reflexivity. }
  split.
  - intro _.
    split.
    + apply mirror_to_rev.
      intros k Hk.
      match goal with
      | Hmirror : forall k, (0 <= k /\ k < q_size_pre) ->
            Znth k lv 0 = Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - k) lv 0,
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv |- _ =>
        apply Hmirror; rewrite Hqlen in Hk; split; lia
      end.
    + rewrite fold_left_Z_add_acc.
      match goal with
      | Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 q_size_pre lv),
        Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
        Hsle : s <= w_pre |- _ =>
        rewrite Hs, (sublist_self lv q_size_pre Hqlen); lia
      end.
  - intros _. reflexivity.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_2 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  assert (Heqi : i = q_size_pre) by lia.
  subst i.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* remaining: problem_72_spec lv w_pre 0 *)
  unfold problem_72_spec.
  split. { left. reflexivity. }
  split.
  - intro H. discriminate H.
  - intro [_ Hfold].
    match goal with
    | Hgt : s > w_pre,
      Hs : s = sum (sublist 0 q_size_pre lv),
      Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv |- _ =>
      rewrite fold_left_Z_add_acc in Hfold;
      rewrite Hs, (sublist_self lv q_size_pre Hqlen) in Hgt;
      lia
    end.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_3 : p072_will_it_fly_return_wit_3.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  entailer!.
  all: try lia.
  all: try assumption.
  (* remaining: problem_72_spec lv w_pre 0 *)
  unfold problem_72_spec.
  split. { left. reflexivity. }
  split.
  - intro H. discriminate H.
  - intro [Hrev _].
    match goal with
    | Hneq : Znth i lv 0 <> Znth (q_size_pre - 1 - i) lv 0,
      Hqlen : q_size_pre = Zlength lv,
      Hilt : i < q_size_pre,
      Hige : 0 <= i |- _ =>
      apply Hneq;
      rewrite Hqlen;
      rewrite <- (Znth_rev_lem lv i ltac:(rewrite <- Hqlen; lia));
      rewrite <- Hrev;
      reflexivity
    end.
Qed.

Lemma Znth_rev_lem : forall (l : list Z) (k : Z),

Lemma mirror_to_rev : forall (lv : list Z),
  (forall k, 0 <= k < Zlength lv -> Znth k lv 0 = Znth (Zlength lv - 1 - k) lv 0) ->
  lv = rev lv.
Proof.

  rewrite (list_eq_ext lv (rev lv) 0).
  split.
  - rewrite !Zlength_correct. rewrite length_rev. reflexivity.
  - intros i Hi.

    apply Hmirror. exact Hi.
Qed.
